inputs.fcf_source: Two-year average FY2023–FY2024 free cash flow per share $0.16 (FY2025 trough cash burn $0.42/sh excluded from base normalization) per AEHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250728_rpt20250530_acc0001654954_25_008553.htm
inputs.normalization_note: FY2024 net income peak $33.2M; FY2025 operating cash outflow $7.4M after SiC burn-in order pause.

bear.notes: Customer concentration persists; cash burn continues

base.notes: Partial SiC / AI burn-in recovery from FY2025 trough

bull.notes: Multi-customer SiC capacity rebuild plus services attach

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Operating equipment manufacturer; no land-at-zero pattern

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / dormant assets?
option_scan.evidence: No hidden real-estate or royalty reserve

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Single reporting segment; FY2025 net loss consolidated

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: Deferred revenue $1.91M current plus noncurrent per AEHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260408_rpt20260227_acc0001654954_26_003348.htm

option_scan.question: Private or illiquid stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: No material private equity stakes disclosed

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution / legal recovery?
option_scan.evidence: No litigation recovery or special dividend catalyst

option_scan.question: Embedded product option in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: SiC wafer-level burn-in and AI accelerator test demand cited in 10-K MD&A

growth_explanation.mechanism: FOX-XP and WaferPak burn-in system shipments drive revenue; high-margin consumables and upgrades follow installed base; SiC and flash-memory capex cycles swing order volume.
growth_explanation.source: AEHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250728_rpt20250530_acc0001654954_25_008553.htm Item 1 business description and MD&A revenue discussion

falsifiers.trigger: Two consecutive fiscal years with revenue below $50M without matching gross margin recovery
Operating cash outflow exceeds $10M for two consecutive fiscal years

component_valuation.coverage_statement: Four additive components map mid-cycle burn-in operations, deferred revenue option, net financial claims, and cycle/concentration reserve once each.

components.label: Mid-cycle burn-in / test systems owner-cash engine

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from AEHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250728_rpt20250530_acc0001654954_25_008553.htm: FY2025 revenue $58.968M, OCF $-7.4M, capex $4.992M, cash $24.529M; contract backfill 2026-07-23.
valuation.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K and Q3 FY2026 10-Q (AEHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260408_rpt20260227_acc0001654954_26_003348.htm) before decision use.
valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or cycle path is materially worse than low case.

inputs.label: FY2025 net cash from operating activities

assumptions.label: Mid-cycle normalized owner free cash flow per diluted share
assumptions.rationale: Two-year average FY2023–FY2024 free cash flow per share anchors mid-cycle; FY2025 trough ($0.42/sh cash burn) bounds bear.

assumptions.label: Growth years 1–5 on normalized owner cash
assumptions.rationale: SiC / AI burn-in recovery vs customer pause after FY2024 peak.

assumptions.label: Growth years 6–7 on normalized owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Long-run fade after implant cycle normalization.

assumptions.label: Selling multiple on year-7 owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Specialty equipment multiple band; bear trough persistence, bull mid-cycle re-rating.

assumptions.label: Discount rate on owner cash path
Schedule adjustment to legacy component targets
assumptions.rationale: Scales raw DCF to filing-grounded component schedule without changing overlap keys.

calculations.label: Systems backlog conversion option per share

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Typical 12-24 month implant system delivery from backlog disclosure (10-K); base realization ~2 years
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Backlog conversion history and FY2025 deferred revenue recognition pace (10-K backlog note)
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Incremental capex for backlog conversion already in normalized owner-cash path

driver_model.timing_basis: Typical 12-24 month implant system delivery from backlog disclosure (10-K); base realization ~2 years
Typical 12-24 month implant system delivery from backlog disclosure (10-K); base realization ~2 years

assumptions.label: Capital-cycle trough, China exposure, and backlog-decline reserve
assumptions.rationale: Negative reserve for WFE downcycle persistence, 84% international/China mix, and backlog falling from $646M to $457M not fully captured in core multiple.

calculations.label: Cycle and concentration reserve per share

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

insider_signal.disclaimer: Context only. Insider activity informs scenario confidence and stance discussion; it does not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

scenario_confidence.note: Scenario weights are qualitative confidence only; Lawrence base IRR unchanged.

component_valuation_results.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

trigger.policy: A complete economic-value analysis is required when any trigger fires; otherwise it is recommended.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of Aehr Test Systems common stock, including mid-cycle burn-in and test operations, deferred revenue conversion option, net cash claims, and cycle or concentration reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Debt-free balance sheet. Mid-cycle operating engine, deferred revenue option, net cash, and cycle reserve are additive with unique overlap keys; no component capitalizes the same claim twice.

economic_value_analysis.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K and Q3 FY2026 10-Q; mid-cycle owner cash normalized on FY2023–FY2024 average free cash flow per share with FY2025 trough excluded from base.

component_groups.label: Mid-cycle burn-in / test systems owner-cash engine
component_groups.economic_claim: Mid-cycle burn-in / test systems owner-cash engine
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on normalized $0.1632 per share mid-cycle free cash flow per AEHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250728_rpt20250530_acc0001654954_25_008553.htm.
component_groups.adjustments: FY2025 trough cash burn ($0.42/sh) bounds bear case; SiC recovery assumptions cite partial nine-month FY2026 net income $8.5M.

component_groups.label: Deferred revenue and customer deposit conversion option
component_groups.economic_claim: Deferred revenue and customer deposit conversion option
component_groups.valuation_basis: Risk-adjusted milestone on $1.91M deferred revenue per Q3 FY2026 10-Q.
component_groups.adjustments: Success probability and timing reflect typical 12–24 month system delivery from backlog disclosure.

component_groups.label: Net cash and financial claims per share
component_groups.economic_claim: Net cash and financial claims per share
component_groups.valuation_basis: Cash $36.9M less no term debt divided by 31.453M diluted shares per Q3 FY2026 10-Q.
component_groups.adjustments: ATM program is modeled in cycle reserve, not net cash.

component_groups.label: Cycle, customer concentration, and dilution reserve
component_groups.economic_claim: Cycle, customer concentration, and dilution reserve
component_groups.valuation_basis: Reserve for customer pause, $60M ATM dilution overhang, and order lumpiness.
component_groups.adjustments: Customer concentration risk disclosed in FY2025 10-K; April 2026 ATM 8-K sets dilution ceiling.

Mid-cycle owner cash normalization remains [HUMAN REVIEW] vs spot trough cash burn.

component_review_queue.decision_rule: Each assigned rater records accept, challenge, or reject with a proposed range and evidence. The aggregator may not silently average disputed ranges.

items.label: Mid-cycle burn-in / test systems owner-cash engine
mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
Is the security's ownership claim and economic-unit denominator reconciled correctly?
Does the evidence support the low/base/high range and its timing?
Are comparable assets truly like-for-like after production, contract, location, capital, ownership, and realization adjustments?
For an option, are success probability, time to realization, and remaining owner-funded capital explicit?
Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?

valuation_method_route.label: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
failure_modes: replacement cost used for assets that cannot earn their cost of capital
valuation_method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

high.rationale: Negative reserve for WFE downcycle persistence, 84% international/China mix, and backlog falling from $646M to $457M not fully captured in core multiple.

high.label: Cycle and concentration reserve per share

economic_ownership_map.evidence: Primary bridge from AEHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250728_rpt20250530_acc0001654954_25_008553.htm: FY2025 revenue $58.968M, OCF $-7.4M, capex $4.992M, cash $24.529M; contract backfill 2026-07-23.
economic_ownership_map.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Customer concentration risk disclosed in FY2025 10-K; April 2026 ATM 8-K sets dilution ceiling.
economic_ownership_map.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or cycle path is materially worse than low case.

method_route.label: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
failure_modes: replacement cost used for assets that cannot earn their cost of capital
method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

top_value_drivers.label: Deferred revenue and customer deposit conversion option
Cycle, customer concentration, and dilution reserve
Mid-cycle burn-in / test systems owner-cash engine
Net cash and financial claims per share

source_lineage.locator: Backlog section: systems backlog including deferred systems revenue $457.0M at December 31, 2025

falsifiers: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or cycle path is materially worse than low case.
Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or cycle path is materially worse than low case.
Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or cycle path is materially worse than low case.
Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or cycle path is materially worse than low case.
required_refresh_triggers: new filing or material operating update

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

universal_valuation_contract.decision_rule: No security is decision-grade until every material claim is valued exactly once by a valid calculation proof and every material evidence blocker is resolved.

paths.label: Growth theory (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of Aehr Test Systems common stock, including mid-cycle burn-in and test operations, deferred revenue conversion option, net cash claims, and cycle or concentration reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Debt-free balance sheet. Mid-cycle operating engine, deferred revenue option, net cash, and cycle reserve are additive with unique overlap keys; no component capitalizes the same claim twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K and Q3 FY2026 10-Q; mid-cycle owner cash normalized on FY2023–FY2024 average free cash flow per share with FY2025 trough excluded from base.

component_groups.label: Mid-cycle burn-in / test systems owner-cash engine
component_groups.economic_claim: Mid-cycle burn-in / test systems owner-cash engine
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on normalized $0.1632 per share mid-cycle free cash flow per AEHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250728_rpt20250530_acc0001654954_25_008553.htm.
component_groups.adjustments: FY2025 trough cash burn ($0.42/sh) bounds bear case; SiC recovery assumptions cite partial nine-month FY2026 net income $8.5M.

component_groups.label: Deferred revenue and customer deposit conversion option
component_groups.economic_claim: Deferred revenue and customer deposit conversion option
component_groups.valuation_basis: Risk-adjusted milestone on $1.91M deferred revenue per Q3 FY2026 10-Q.
component_groups.adjustments: Success probability and timing reflect typical 12–24 month system delivery from backlog disclosure.

component_groups.label: Net cash and financial claims per share
component_groups.economic_claim: Net cash and financial claims per share
component_groups.valuation_basis: Cash $36.9M less no term debt divided by 31.453M diluted shares per Q3 FY2026 10-Q.
component_groups.adjustments: ATM program is modeled in cycle reserve, not net cash.

component_groups.label: Cycle, customer concentration, and dilution reserve
component_groups.economic_claim: Cycle, customer concentration, and dilution reserve
component_groups.valuation_basis: Reserve for customer pause, $60M ATM dilution overhang, and order lumpiness.
component_groups.adjustments: Customer concentration risk disclosed in FY2025 10-K; April 2026 ATM 8-K sets dilution ceiling.

limitations: Component schedule is filing-grounded but not committee-approved.
Mid-cycle owner cash normalization remains [HUMAN REVIEW] vs spot trough cash burn.

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory lens blend only. Does not replace Lawrence total-synthesis stance gate without [HUMAN REVIEW].

pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: Per-share claims use fully diluted shares from valuation.inputs.
pricing_analysis.implied_growth_contract: Constant 7-year owner-cash growth with a 14x terminal owner-cash multiple; diagnostic, not forecast.
falsifiers: replacement cost used for assets that cannot earn their cost of capital
pricing_analysis.pricing_conclusion: Entry prices were computed mechanically from the routed power-zone profile (Capital cycle and normalized industry economics). They are decision inputs, not a decision; the owner must review the scenarios before acting.

trigger.policy: A complete economic-value analysis is required when any trigger fires; otherwise it is recommended.
